feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unable to Find “Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting-Specific” in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently working on Asset Accounting configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

However, we are unable to locate the configuration step “Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting-Specific” in the system.

entry.title: Mass change in the Purchase Scheduling Agreement Schedule Lines
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

I would like to raise a ticket regarding a mass change requirement in the Purchase scheduling agreement schedule lines within the public cloud environment. The issue involves updating multiple schedule lines simultaneously, and we are seeking guidance or support on how to execute this efficiently.

Please advise on the process or provide assistance in implementing this change.

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Unable to fix plant wise profit center and Cost center for small price difference GL/ round off GL
entry.content:
Unable to fix plant wise profit center and Cost center for small price difference GL/ round off GL while doing MIRO  - App Create Supplier Invoice Advanced 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to map ECC SPRO/IMG Activities to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SSCUIs - Looking for pract
entry.content:
Body:
Hi Community,
I'm working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation project where we are migrating from ECC 6.0. During the Fit-to-Standard (F2S) workshops, I need to create mapping documentation that shows:

ECC side: SPRO path / IMG Activity ID / Transaction Code
S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition side: SSCUI ID / Fiori App name / Configuration path

My Goal:
Create a comprehensive mapping table for FI module scope items (e.g., Accounts Receivable, Credit Management, General Ledger, etc.) to support:

Gap analysis documentation
BDCQ (Business-Driven Configuration Questionnaire) creation
Knowledge transfer from ECC consultants to Cloud consultants

What I've found so far:

SAP Notes mention "Configuration Activity.xlsm (SAP Customer)" in Roadmap Viewer
Some community posts reference the CUS_IMGACH table for reverse lookup
But I haven't found a ready-made, publicly available mapping document

My Questions:
1. Does such a mapping document exist?

Is there an official SAP document/spreadsheet that maps ECC IMG Activities to S/4HANA Cloud SSCUIs?
Or is "Configuration Activity.xlsm" the closest thing available?

2. If I need to use Configuration Activity.xlsm:

Step 1: Where exactly in Roadmap Viewer do I download it? (Which Roadmap? Which section?)
Step 2: Which sheet(s) should I look at? (I heard "IMG_Act BusinessCatalog Match" sheet exists)
Step 3: How do I search/filter to find SSCUI for a specific ECC IMG Activity or SPRO path?
Step 4: Can I export/pivot this data to create my own mapping table?

3. Alternative approaches:

Are there any SAP Help Portal pages that document SSCUI↔IMG relationships by module (FI/CO/SD/MM)?
Can I use Best Practice Explorer to find this mapping?
Should I check CBC (Central Business Configuration) documentation instead?
Any community-maintained spreadsheets or GitHub repos with this mapping?

4. Specific example:

ECC: SPRO → Financial Accounting → Accounts Receivable → Business Transactions → Outgoing Payments → Automatic Outgoing Payments → Payment Methods per Company Code
What is the equivalent SSCUI ID / Fiori App in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Background:
I'm an SAP FI consultant with strong ECC/on-premise experience but new to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. I can navigate SPRO blindfolded, but the Fiori-based configuration approach is still new to me.
Any concrete, actionable guidance with screenshots or step-by-step instructions would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks in advance for your help!
Best regards

entry.title: In Manage Production Orders, the column “Order Text” remains empty
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and noticed that the column “Order Text” in the app Manage Production Orders remains empty, even though I have maintained header text / long text / short text on the production order.

Could you please clarify which exact field or text source is used to fill this column?
Is this column linked to a specific order description field rather than the maintained production order texts?

Additionally, is there any standard way in the capacity planning apps to display such production order long texts or shift notes directly for the orders?

Kind regards,

Jana

entry.title: Order confirmation CDS View
entry.content:
1.For the Order Confirmation output print from Sales order, the “Tax Classification for Customer 1” field is available in CDS view I_SalesDocument, but the data/value is not available in CDS view I_SalesDocument.

2. From which CDS view can we retrieve the Tax Code, Sales office mobile phone number and Payment Due Date.

All the above-mentioned data, such as Tax Classification Customer 1 and Tax Code, are available in Sales Order 268 on the DEV system. The Sales Office mobile phone number is maintained in the master data and the Payment Due Date from which CDS view we can retrieve. Please let us know the update.

entry.title: Payment methods are not inherited from selected open items in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app
entry.content:
Dear All,

We are using the “Clear Incoming Payments” app to clear open items.

When we select original open items for offsetting/clearing, we expected some payment-related fields from the selected original line items to be inherited into the clearing/payment document. However, the following three fields are not inherited from the selected open items:

  • Payment Method
  • Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation
  • Cash Discount Days 1

Our main question is:

Is there any standard configuration, app setting, BAdI, substitution, or other enhancement option that allows these three fields to be inherited from the selected original open items during clearing in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app?

We would like to know whether SAP standard behavior intentionally does not copy these fields during clearing, or whether there is a way to control the field transfer.

The important point is that we are not simply asking why the fields are blank.
We specifically want to know whether there is any possible solution to make the following fields inherit from the selected source open item:

  1. Payment Method
  2. Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation
  3. Cash Discount Days 1

If this is standard behavior and these fields cannot be inherited in this app, we would also appreciate confirmation and any recommended workaround.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Missing “Selection Parameters” in PROD for Fiori App
entry.content:
Body:

Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

In the Fiori application (Schedule Accounting Data Changes / related app), we can see the “Selection Parameters” section in the DEV / configuration environment. However, in the PROD system, this section is missing.

In PROD, only the execution screen is displayed, and it is not possible to maintain or view selection parameters.

We would like to confirm the following points:

  • Is this expected standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • If yes, where are these selection parameters stored and maintain

entry.title: change of Payment format for sales(customer incoming payments)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

 

As we wanted to change payment format for customer payments where excatly we change the format in the sap public cloud .when we upload new bank statements it should allow new format that is required to accept the payments

entry.title: Verification of Purchasing Group determination logic for manual PR/PO (Reference: KBA 3728011)
entry.content:
When creating a Purchase Requisition (PR) or Purchase Order (PO) manually in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system automatically proposes Purchasing Group "001" from the Material Master, instead of "002" from the Purchasing Info Record.
what is the standard behavior for manual PRs and POs is for the Purchasing Group from the Material Master to take priority.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: I want to know how to resolve real-time revenue errors.
entry.content:
An error occurs stating that real-time posting is not supported when assigning accounts to context assets. I would like to know how to resolve this. 

While reprocessing with real-time revenue recognition allows revenue recognition to run, this error appears every month, so I want to resolve it.

Work procedure

1.Register client projects based on their progress. 

2. Register the account category while linking it to the customer project.

3.Register fixed assets

4.Register a supplier invoice.

5.In the supplier invoice approval workflow, the approver edits the details of the supplier invoice. The edit involves setting fixed assets to the details of the supplier invoice for the order registered in the project's account assignment.

6.Complete the supplier invoice approval flow.

7.Perform revenue recognition

entry.title: Storage location determination for delivery
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to configure stock determination for sales delivery. As part of it, we have used the standard stock determination group 001 and assigned it to the material and also the stock determination rule 001 to movement type 601 and also for the item category TAN.

 But even after this, the system is not automatically determining the storage location in neither in the outbound document or while posting for goods issue. 

our requirement is that we have already enabled batch determination. but during batch determination, it is fetching the batches from all storage locations under the plant. we want to restrict this to certain list of storage locations. 

we also understand that there is a standard storage location determination for SD but that gives us the option only to assign one storage location for a shipping point+plant+storage condition combination. we actually have a list of storage locations from which batch determination is allowed.

please guide on what are the possible solutions that we are missing out on. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: The Credit Exposure is not updated.
entry.content:
The Credit Exposure is not updated after creating Sales Orde. We have activated Scope Item BD6.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Change "Open on Key date" in the "Overdue receivables" app
entry.content:
Hello,

is it possible to enter a specific date in the app "Overdue receivables" in the "Open on key date" field?

Or is it just possible to choose between TODAY, TOMORROW, YESTERDAY, FIRSTDAYOFTHEYEAR,...

Kind regards

Fabian

entry.title: Substitution Journal Entries - requirement for WBS
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to substitute all postings to the realised and unrealised foreign exchange (RXD/UXD) GL accounts to a specific cost centre per Joint Venture & Equity Group.

Ideally, we would use the substitution for this. but we have an issue when the original posting is not to a cost centre (to a WBS).  If you leave WBS out of the Precondition, the cost centre will be posted to as well as the WBS as there is no mechanism to clear the WBS. Standard SAP posting rules will lead to the WBS getting the rule posting and the CC receiving a statistical posting. This is not desirable.

Ideal Solution currently not available in Public Cloud: To add WBS Element as a field to be used as a Substitution Target Field, then clear the WBS value and post to the substituted Cost Centre.  As confirmed by SAP Support (and Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence), this is currently not available in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Could you please share any workaround on this? Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Migration/Cut-over handling for open 107/109 Goods-in-Transit process when 107 and invoice are post.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

we are currently using the Goods-in-Transit / Origin Acceptance process with movement types 107 and 109, as described in the SAP blog “Goods in transit using 107 & 109 mvt types”.

The business scenario is import procurement with long lead times, for example containers shipped by sea. Financial ownership of the goods is transferred before the goods physically arrive at the receiving plant. Therefore, in the legacy system we already post the first goods receipt step with movement type 107. This creates the valuated stock / valuated GR blocked stock in transit and also creates the accounting document. The supplier invoice is also already posted and completed in the legacy system.

During cut-over, however, some of these goods will still be physically in transit. After go-live, the physical receipt at the warehouse should happen in the new SAP S/4HANA system. In the normal process this would be the second step with movement type 109, releasing the stock from valuated GR blocked stock / stock in transit into available stock.

The challenge is that the first step, movement type 107, and the invoice have already been completed in the legacy system. Only the physical receipt step is still open and should be executed in the new system.

My question is:

What is the recommended migration and cut-over approach for this situation?

More specifically:

  1. Should the open 107/109 items be migrated into the new system as open valuated GR blocked stock / stock in transit, so that movement type 109 can be posted after go-live?
  2. How should the accounting side be handled, considering that the FI posting from movement type 107 and the supplier invoice already exist in the legacy system and should not be duplicated in the new system?
  3. Is there a SAP-recommended standard approach for such cut-over cases, or is a workaround/custom migration approach usually required?
  4. Would it be better to complete both 107 and 109 in the legacy system before cut-over and migrate the stock as normal unrestricted stock only after physical arrival, or is it feasible to technically continue the open 107/109 process across systems?

Any experience, recommended migration design, or lessons learned from similar S/4HANA cut-over projects would be very helpful.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Why is report DE_AUDIT_EUBP (DRC) only valid from 1 January 2024?
entry.content:
Hi,

my customer needs to provide the legal report DE_AUDIT_EUBP for the fiscal year 2022. But when trying to set up the actitivites, I can only choose "Valid From" 2024 and it is not possible to change. 

Is there any way to use the report for earlier fiscal years?  If not, what's the alternative?

Thank you and best regards

Rado

entry.title: Cash Journal
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

Could you please suggest any standard cash journal report available in SAP Public Cloud for branch-wise consolidated reporting.

Currently, we only have the following two apps available for Cash Journal processing:

  • Post Cash Journal Entries
  • Cash Journal

However, as per the customer’s requirement, they need a branch-wise consolidated Cash Journal report. At present, we are unable to find any option or filter in the standard apps to generate the report branch-wise.

Kindly suggest if there is any standard report, or alternative solution available for branch-wise consolidated Cash Journal reporting in SAP Public Cloud.

Thanks

entry.title: Not able to add a new Routine Number inside the Pricing Procedure in the Drop down Group Condition
entry.content:
In the Above Screen shot we need a new Routine Number which can take some other Material Group into consider than what Sap standard has defined.

we have tried Via Config we are not able to find such Options in S4 HANA Public Cloud , we used to do in On Prem where we can Copy R Program into Z ones and then we used to achieve such Requirements now In S4 HANA Public cloud we are not able to see such options.

We have Tried the below 5  Cloud BADIS for S4  HANA Public cloud we do not have changing Parameters for changing the Default Material Group

  • PRCG_DOC_CONDITION_AMOUNT : Define Alternative Calculation of Condition Amount in Document
  • PRCG_DOC_ITEM_CONDITION_AMOUNT: Define Alternative Calculation of Condition Amount in Document Item
  • PRCG_DOC_ITM_CNDN_BASE_VALUE: Define Alternative Calculation of Condition Base Value in Document Item
  • PRICING_DOCUMENT_REQUIREMENT: Define Requirement in Pricing for Document
  • PRCG_DOCUMENT_ITEM_REQUIREMENT: Define Requirement in Pricing for Document Item

    if any body has done such requirement for Pricing Please let us Know or at least give us Direction how can we achieve such requirement

entry.title: Error creating Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are experiencing a error when creating Purchase Requisitions.

The problem occurs with certain materials that have a price of 0 in the material master data (transaction MM03). These materials are intentionally maintained with a zero price because they are non-stock managed materials.

According to the Performance Assistant / field help, the price field should not be mandatory. However, in practice, the system requires the field to be filled in order to continue.

When we leave the field empty, the system does not allow us to proceed. If we enter 0 as the price, the system throws an error and the Purchase Requisition cannot be submitted.

We would like to know:

  • Is there any configuration or parameterization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that allows Purchase Requisitions to be created with a price of 0?
  • How can this behavior be adjusted so that materials with a permanent zero price can be processed correctly?
  • Is there any standard configuration or best practice recommended by SAP for this scenario?

Thank you in advance.

Greetings
